summary: Stunning soundboard recording with crystal‑clear audio and an all‑time great 1987 MSG show highlighted by a legendary Morning Dew and the Good Lovin → La Bamba → Good Lovin sandwich.
review: The MattMan remaster of the 9/18/87 Madison Square Garden set is widely praised for its pristine soundboard quality – many call it the best‑sounding copy available. Musically the concert is a fan favorite: a short but punchy first set gives way to a second set that erupts with Shakedown, Terrapin, Watchtower and an unforgettable Morning Dew topped by the Good Lovin/La Bamba medley. Jerry Garcia’s guitar work and vocals are described as “on fire,” Brent’s keyboards sparkle (though a few note his voice sits low in the mix), and the rhythm section drives the band with relentless energy.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 60 reviews, the vast majority 5‑star, repeatedly praise both performance and audio quality; only a handful of mixed or low comments exist, so the combined show (70 %) + recording (30 %) score is near‑perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, clear and the best‑sounding copy; a few notes about Brent’s vocal level being low in the mix but no dropouts or major flaws.

standout_songs: Good Lovin > La Bamba > Good Lovin
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is short but tight; the second set launches with Shakedown and builds through Terrapin, Watchtower and an explosive Dew. Transitions (e.g., Good Lovin → La Bamba) are highlighted as seamless, creating a high‑energy arc that many reviewers call “the best second set of the era.”

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar solo on the Dew and his vocal performance (including the La Bamba interlude) are repeatedly called perfect, fiery and emotionally cathartic.
band_member_comments.Phil: Generally solid bass work; one reviewer notes “Phil's good anyway,” no major complaints.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir gets kudos for a wild intro (“levitate” joke) and strong rhythm playing, though equipment hiccups during Birdsong are mentioned in one review.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s B‑3 organ and backup vocals receive high praise; a couple of reviewers note his vocal presence is lower than usual in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart are lauded for powerful, “Bonham‑like” drumming that drives songs like Hell in a Bucket and Watchtower.
